mapping.a_element: interaction based on topological distance (fixed number of neighbors)
mapping.b_element: interaction matrix defined through a set of patterns (fixed rank, independent of system size N)

mapping.a_element: maintaining cohesion against density changes (external perturbations)
mapping.b_element: inference stability against sampling noise and system size variations
fixed rank of interaction matrix (much smaller than N)
statistical mechanics techniques validating pattern corrections

brief_justification: While both papers involve interactions constrained by non-metric properties (topological distance in A, pattern rank in B), their core mechanisms differ fundamentally. Paper A studies emergent collective behavior via local topological rules in a physical system, whereas Paper B focuses on inferring high-dimensional interactions in a statistical model using Principal Component Analysis and corrections derived from the Hopfield model. The shared concept of 'fixed constraints' does not translate to the same underlying machinery.
